title: OpenSlides
description: Web-based presentation and assembly system for managing and projecting agendas, motions, elections and live voting at meetings.
installation: OpenSlides 4 is a microservice stack and CANNOT run from this single image alone; this template deploys the core application server (openslides-backend, action API on container port 9002, presenter on 9003) plus its PostgreSQL (the datastore) and Valkey/Redis (message bus + autoupdate) add-ons. You MUST also deploy the companion services as separate apps and place the proxy in front as the public web entrypoint: openslides/openslides-proxy (HTTP entrypoint, listens on 8000), openslides/openslides-client (Angular frontend), openslides/openslides-datastore-reader and openslides/openslides-datastore-writer (datastore over PostgreSQL), openslides/openslides-auth (login/session, needs Valkey), openslides/openslides-autoupdate (real-time push, needs Valkey), openslides/openslides-vote (electronic voting, needs PostgreSQL + Valkey), openslides/openslides-icc (chat/notify), openslides/openslides-media (uploaded files/PDFs), openslides/openslides-search (full-text, optional), and openslides/openslides-manage-service (one-shot initial-data + superadmin bootstrap). All services share secrets via the AUTH_TOKEN_KEY / AUTH_COOKIE_KEY / internal auth password keys - generate them once with 'openssl rand -hex 32' and set the SAME value across every service; rotate the placeholder values below and the postgres add-on password before production. Run openslides-manage 'initial-data' + 'set-password' once to create the first superadmin (default login admin/admin - change immediately). Point all services at postgres host 'openslides-postgresql-rw' and redis host 'rfr-openslides-valkey-readwrite'. See https://github.com/OpenSlides/OpenSlides/blob/main/DEVELOPMENT.md for the full compose/manifest of every container.
